project.name: course-dev-ide-with-agents
project.description: Collaborative intelligence infrastructure for course content production — a multi-agent orchestrator (Marcus) driving a per-specialist gated LangGraph scaffold with tamper-evident HIL operator verdicts, built on LangChain/LangGraph.

nodes.name: resume_api.py
nodes.summary: Gate resume API that registers operator decision cards, computes their digests, and rebuilds run state from an operator verdict to resume a paused LangGraph run.

nodes.name: dispatch_adapter.py
nodes.summary: Production dispatch adapter that the Marcus orchestrator uses to compile manifests and dispatch specialist sub-graphs against the shared run state.

nodes.name: gate_runner.py

nodes.languageNotes: Orchestrates LangChain chat-model invocation with caching and structured JSON payload parsing.

nodes.name: directive_model.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 models for the Section 02A directive (DirectiveSource, Directive) with role/excluded-reason enums and strict field invariants for UUID4, tz-aware timestamps, and role-dependent fields.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 with TypeAdapter literal validation, field validators, and legacy-key migration.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Section 02A gate, re-exporting the poll-surface submit/resume API for transport adapters.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package __init__ acting as a re-export barrel.

nodes.name: _transports.py
nodes.summary: Transport adapters (CLI, HTTP, MCP stdio) that submit an operator verdict through the shared poll-surface and return the displayed directive plus resume payload.

nodes.name: poll_surface.py
nodes.summary: Human-in-the-loop poll surface for the Section 02A gate: loads/coerces directives, computes canonical model digests, applies operator edits, and builds and resumes from operator verdicts.
nodes.languageNotes: Computes deterministic SHA-256 digests over canonicalized Pydantic JSON for verdict integrity.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_02a.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 models for the Section 02A operator verdict and directive edit payload, enforcing UUID4, tz-aware timestamps, SHA-256 digest format, and verb/payload consistency.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 model validators enforcing cross-field verb/payload invariants.

nodes.name: ComposerCache
nodes.summary: Simple dict-backed cache mapping prompt hashes to LLM response text for the duration of a composition run.

nodes.name: render_classification_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders the per-file corpus classification prompt from a Jinja template, injecting relative path, file stats, and a binary hex sample.

nodes.name: compose_and_write
nodes.summary: Resolves the chat model, runs composition, writes the model-resolution trail JSON, and persists the directive YAML with a SHA-256 integrity digest.

nodes.name: compose
nodes.summary: Main composition routine that walks the corpus, classifies each non-excluded file via the LLM, and assembles a validated Directive.

nodes.name: _classify_with_llm
nodes.summary: Classifies a single corpus file by rendering a prompt, consulting the cache, invoking the LLM on miss, and parsing the JSON payload into a DirectiveSource.

nodes.name: write_directive_yaml
nodes.summary: Serializes a Directive to YAML and writes it to disk, creating parent directories as needed.

nodes.name: DirectiveSource
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for one corpus source entry with legacy-key migration and validators enforcing known roles, excluded reasons, and role-dependent field invariants.

nodes.name: Directive
nodes.summary: Top-level Section 02A directive model holding run metadata and source list, enforcing UUID4 run IDs and tz-aware composition timestamps.

nodes.name: submit_cli_verdict
nodes.summary: CLI transport that submits an operator verdict to the poll-surface and returns the transport response.

nodes.name: submit_http_verdict
nodes.summary: HTTP transport that submits an operator verdict to the poll-surface and returns the transport response.

nodes.name: submit_mcp_stdio_verdict
nodes.summary: MCP stdio transport that submits an operator verdict to the poll-surface and returns the transport response.

nodes.name: submit_verdict
nodes.summary: Builds a validated Section02AOperatorVerdict from operator input, coercing the directive, applying edits, and binding the decision-card digest.

nodes.name: resume_from_verdict
nodes.summary: Resumes the pipeline from an operator verdict, re-validating the directive digest and producing the resumed directive plus verdict payloads.

nodes.name: apply_directive_edit
nodes.summary: Applies an operator's per-source field edits to the directive, rejecting unknown ref IDs or fields and re-validating the resulting model.

nodes.name: display_directive
nodes.summary: Coerces and renders a directive for operator review, attaching its computed decision-card digest.

nodes.name: Section02AOperatorVerdict
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for an operator's gate verdict, enforcing UUID4 IDs, tz-aware timestamps, SHA-256 digest format, and verb/payload consistency.

nodes.name: DirectiveEditPayload

nodes.name: env.py
nodes.summary: Builds the Jinja2 Environment for the v4.2 pack generator, wiring a FileSystemLoader to the template root so section/layout templates can be resolved at render time.

nodes.name: manifest.py

nodes.languageNotes: Uses dataclass-style typed models and frozenset-based exclusion filtering over the graph manifest.

nodes.name: render.py

nodes.name: make_env
nodes.summary: Constructs and returns a Jinja2 Environment whose FileSystemLoader points at the resolved template root directory.

nodes.name: MissingSectionTemplateError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when a manifest step has no matching section template under the template root.

nodes.name: GeneratorStep

nodes.name: GeneratorManifest
nodes.summary: Typed model aggregating the resolved generator steps with schema version, pack version, and generator reference.

nodes.name: _template_name
nodes.summary: Resolves the section template filename for a step by globbing the sections directory and matching a slugified label, raising MissingSectionTemplateError when none is found.

nodes.name: _module_path

nodes.name: _orchestration_only_ids

nodes.name: load_generator_manifest

nodes.name: render_pack
nodes.summary: Renders the v4.2 pack markdown by loading the generator manifest and workflow policy, building the Jinja2 env, rendering the layout template, and writing the result to the output path.

nodes.name: render_parity_evidence_markdown

nodes.name: _flatten_mapping
nodes.summary: Recursively flattens a nested mapping into sorted dotted-key lines for deterministic line-based comparison.

nodes.name: _compare_semantic_lines
nodes.summary: Compares two flattened mappings line-by-line, returning a match score, counts, and a divergence rationale.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses arguments, runs the parity comparison, and writes the Markdown evidence report.

nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of Pydantic v2 strict mode (extra='forbid', validate_assignment, strict) with field/model validators to enforce dev-lane-only thread namespaces and topology integrity.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Cora handler package init exposing the shared append_checkpoint helper that builds a NodeCheckpoint with SanctumFingerprint and merges it into StoryState.

nodes.name: implement_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the implement_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: plan_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the plan_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: review_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the review_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: test_story.py
nodes.summary: Cora dev-graph handler for the test_story node; appends a checkpoint via the shared helper.

nodes.name: node_checkpoint.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic v2 NodeCheckpoint model capturing per-node story progress with timezone-aware timestamps, status enum, and an embedded sanctum fingerprint.

nodes.name: sanctum_fingerprint.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic v2 SanctumFingerprint model recording a UUID4 snapshot id, SHA-256 content digest, and timezone-aware capture time for sanctum state snapshots.

nodes.name: story_state.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic v2 StoryState model representing the LangGraph dev-lane story-scoped state with a list of node checkpoints and updated_at timestamp.

nodes.name: node_checkpoint_validators.py
nodes.summary: Cross-field validator enforcing the NodeCheckpoint invariant that a 'complete' status requires a completed_at timestamp and non-complete statuses must leave it unset.

nodes.name: _load_preclosure_hook
nodes.summary: Dynamically loads the preclosure hook module from the bmad-agent-cora skills directory using importlib.util, returning the PreClosureResult class and run_preclosure_check function.

nodes.name: block_mode_node
nodes.summary: LangGraph node that runs the Cora pre-closure check against StoryState and raises a GateError when closure is denied; otherwise updates updated_at.

nodes.name: DevGraphNodeSpec
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic model describing a single Cora dev-graph node with id, label, and dotted handler reference.

nodes.name: DevGraphEdgeSpec
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic model describing one directed edge in the dev-graph manifest, aliasing 'from' to from_node to avoid the Python keyword.

nodes.name: DevGraphManifest
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic manifest for the Cora dev-lane graph that validates schema version, node/edge topology, and enforces that thread_namespace targets the dev/ lane with a {story_id} placeholder.

nodes.name: CompiledGraphHandle
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass bundling a compiled LangGraph graph, its source manifest, and a thread-namespace template with a per-story formatter method.

nodes.name: _resolve_handler
nodes.summary: Resolves a dotted 'module:attr' handler reference into a callable, raising CompileError on bad format, missing module/attribute, or non-callable target.

nodes.name: load_dev_graph_manifest
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the dev-graph YAML manifest from disk, raising ManifestValidationError for missing files, malformed YAML, or schema violations.

nodes.name: _build_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs a LangGraph StateGraph[StoryState] by adding each manifest node with its resolved handler and wiring edges, defaulting to START when no explicit start edge exists.

nodes.name: compile_dev_graph
nodes.summary: Top-level entry that coerces a manifest input (path or model), builds the StateGraph, compiles it, and returns a CompiledGraphHandle.

nodes.name: append_checkpoint
nodes.summary: Builds a complete NodeCheckpoint (with SanctumFingerprint derived from a SHA-256 of node_id) and returns the StoryState patch appending it plus refreshing updated_at.

nodes.name: NodeCheckpoint
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model representing one node's checkpoint with status enum, mandatory tz-aware timestamps, optional completed_at, and a model-level invariant requiring completed_at iff status is 'complete'.

nodes.name: SanctumFingerprint
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model carrying a UUID4 snapshot_id, 64-char SHA-256 content_sha256, and tz-aware captured_at, with field validators enforcing UUID version and timezone-awareness.

nodes.name: StoryState
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model for the LangGraph dev-lane state keyed by story_id, holding the ordered list of NodeCheckpoint entries and a tz-aware updated_at.

nodes.name: enforce_complete_requires_completed_at
nodes.summary: Cross-field validator that raises ValueError when status=='complete' is missing completed_at, or when a non-complete status carries a completed_at value.

nodes.name: verdict.py

nodes.name: emitter.py

nodes.languageNotes: Uses psycopg connection + JSONB payload; idempotency key short-circuits duplicate inserts to the same row.

nodes.name: events.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 ledger-event schemas (VerdictLedgerEvent, OverrideLedgerEvent, SanctumMutationLedgerEvent) with UUID4/tz-aware validators, canonical natural-key derivation, and SHA-256 idempotency-key computation.
nodes.languageNotes: Inheritance hierarchy under _LedgerEventBase; idempotency_key derived deterministically from natural_key via hashlib.sha256.

nodes.name: queries.py
nodes.summary: Read-side helpers for the learning ledger: reject-rate-per-gate aggregation, gate inventory listing, and sanctum-mutation event hydration with Pydantic deserialization.
nodes.languageNotes: All queries accept an optional pre-opened psycopg connection to support transactional tests.

nodes.name: gate_decide.py
nodes.summary: MCP-server tool wrappers (gate_decide_tool, override_submit_tool, override_apply_tool) that forward operator verdicts and override flows through the same resume/override APIs the HTTP endpoint uses.

nodes.name: override_api.py
nodes.summary: In-process override registry and orchestration: computes cache-invalidation impact, mints confirm tokens, applies operator verdicts to RunState, and emits OverrideLedgerEvent records to the ledger.
nodes.languageNotes: Module-level registries (_RUN_STATE_REGISTRY, _PENDING_WARNINGS, _CONSUMED_TOKENS) act as test-resettable in-memory state; SHA-256 confirm tokens bind warning to apply call.

nodes.name: override_warning.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 ModelOverrideWarning schema carrying cache-impact estimate, affected-node set, confirm token, and validated expires_at>issued_at invariant.

nodes.name: sanctum_watcher.py
nodes.summary: Filesystem watcher (watchdog + polling fallback) that detects sanctum markdown mutations during an active trial invocation, emits SanctumMutationLedgerEvent records, and queues pending SanctumWarning entries per trial.
nodes.languageNotes: Threading-based polling thread complements watchdog observer; SHA-256 hashes normalize CRLF to LF for cross-platform stability.

nodes.name: assert_scheduler_modules_not_loaded
nodes.summary: Raises SchedulerImportError if any scheduler-prefixed module is already present in sys.modules at import time.

nodes.name: assert_no_scheduler_or_bypass_source
nodes.summary: AST-walks a source string to reject scheduler/bypass imports and unauthorized-bypass-call patterns, raising SchedulerImportError or UnauthorizedResumeBypassError.

nodes.name: gate_verdict_endpoint
nodes.summary: Validates an OperatorVerdict payload, resumes the workflow, and builds the transport response for the verdict endpoint.

nodes.name: gate_override_submit_endpoint
nodes.summary: Forwards an override submission request to submit_override and returns the resulting ModelOverrideWarning as a JSON dump.

nodes.name: gate_override_apply_endpoint
nodes.summary: Applies an override verdict via apply_override and returns the resulting OverrideEvent together with refreshed decision-card metadata.

nodes.name: create_gate_app
nodes.summary: FastAPI app factory that wires the three gate POST routes with operator-id header injection and HTTPException-based error mapping.

nodes.name: EmissionResult
nodes.summary: Frozen result record returned by emit_ledger_event capturing status (inserted/duplicate/failed), event_id, idempotency_key, and failure reason.

nodes.name: emit_ledger_event
nodes.summary: Inserts a ledger event into Postgres with idempotency-key deduplication, schema bootstrap, JSONB payload serialization, and failure-counter increments on errors.

nodes.name: ensure_ledger_schema
nodes.summary: Executes the bundled ledger schema DDL against the provided connection to lazily create the ledger table.

nodes.name: _load_existing_event_id
nodes.summary: Looks up an existing ledger event_id for a given idempotency_key to support deduplicated inserts.

nodes.name: _LedgerEventBase
nodes.summary: Abstract Pydantic base for ledger events providing UUID4/tz-aware validators, payload-extraction, and SHA-256 idempotency_key derivation from a subclass-defined natural_key.

nodes.name: VerdictLedgerEvent
nodes.summary: Ledger event recording an operator gate verdict with verb, card_id, verdict_id, operator_id, and transport_kind metadata.

nodes.name: OverrideLedgerEvent
nodes.summary: Ledger event recording an operator model-override action across the submit/apply phases with confirm_token linkage.

nodes.name: SanctumMutationLedgerEvent
nodes.summary: Ledger event recording a detected mutation of a sanctum markdown file with before/after SHA-256 hashes and suggested invalidating commit.

nodes.name: build_verdict_ledger_event
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a VerdictLedgerEvent from an OperatorVerdict instance and the originating transport kind.

nodes.name: build_override_ledger_event
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs an OverrideLedgerEvent for either the submit or apply phase of a model-override action.

nodes.name: build_sanctum_mutation_ledger_event
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs a SanctumMutationLedgerEvent for a detected file mutation.

nodes.name: reject_rate_per_gate

nodes.name: errors.py

nodes.name: tripwire_ledger.py

nodes.languageNotes: Applies the project Pydantic-v2 schema checklist: validate_assignment=True, extra='forbid', TypeAdapter-based explicit coercion-path red-rejection on the closed Literal alias, and field validators that delegate tz/UUID4 enforcement to app.models.state._base helpers.

nodes.name: verify_audit_chain
nodes.summary: Iterates over an ordered list of TripwireLedgerEntry rows and raises AuditChainOrderError on non-monotonic fired_at per tripwire_id or AuditChainParentLinkError when a fired/marginal-fired verdict lacks a trace_id.

nodes.name: AuditChainIntegrityError
nodes.summary: Root exception type for audit-chain integrity violations (FR-7c-50); parent of the ordering and parent-link error subclasses.

nodes.name: AuditChainOrderError
nodes.summary: Raised when a TripwireLedgerEntry breaks monotonic fired_at ordering for its tripwire_id in the ledger.

nodes.name: AuditChainParentLinkError
nodes.summary: Raised when a fired or marginal-fired verdict row is missing the parent trace_id required for cross-trial provenance.

nodes.name: TripwireId
nodes.summary: Closed StrEnum of the six Slab 7c tripwire identifiers (TW-7c-1 through TW-7c-6) used as a typed key in the ledger schema.

nodes.name: TripwireSeverity
nodes.summary: Closed StrEnum of severity tiers (critical / high / medium) attached to each TripwireLedgerEntry.

nodes.name: TripwireLedgerEntry
nodes.summary: Canonical Pydantic-v2 BaseModel for tripwire-ledger rows: enforces closed tripwire_id via TypeAdapter coercion-path red-rejection, tz-aware fired_at, optional UUID4 trace_id, plus structured severity, verdict, and decision-record fields per Murat A5 / NFR-7c-OD2.
nodes.languageNotes: ConfigDict pins validate_assignment=True and extra='forbid'; pre-mode validator on tripwire_id implements the explicit triple-layer red-rejection idiom from the Pydantic-v2 schema checklist.

nodes.name: main

nodes.name: run_step

tour.title: Project Overview & Agent Roster
tour.description: Start at the skills/ index, the closest thing this project has to a front door: it catalogs every installed BMAD agent skill and shows the cast of characters this codebase orchestrates. Read it alongside the skills README to understand the project's mission, collaborative intelligence infrastructure that turns source material into finished course content through a team of specialist agents. This top-level map names who does what before you meet any code.

tour.title: Meet Marcus, the Orchestrator
tour.description: Marcus is the single operator-facing orchestrator who routes every piece of work to the right specialist and drives the gated run. His SKILL.md encodes the Three Laws, session protocol, and the specialist-registry routing that makes him the project's conductor. Everything downstream in this tour is something Marcus dispatches, gates, or assembles, so understanding his role frames the whole architecture.

tour.title: The Shared State Spine
tour.description: Building on the agent roster, this is the data contract that lets independent agents collaborate without stepping on each other. RunState is the central Pydantic v2 model threaded through every specialist LangGraph as the shared run context, and _base.py holds the reusable validators (timezone-aware datetimes, UUID4 enforcement) that every model reuses. With 39 and 49 dependents respectively, these are the two most depended-upon files in the codebase, so reading them early unlocks everything else.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic v2 models centralize validation in reusable field/model validators. Setting validate_assignment=True and enforcing tz-aware datetimes and UUID4 versions at the base layer means every model that inherits or composes these contracts gets the same red-rejection behavior for free.

tour.title: Tamper-Evident DecisionCards
tour.description: Human-in-the-loop gates are the heart of this system, and DecisionCards are how operator verdicts are recorded with integrity. The base card models carry metadata, party-mode contributions, and the verdict vocabulary, while the package README explains the per-gate (G0-G6) schema family and frozen-hash discipline. Connecting to Step 3's state spine, these cards are the structured artifacts the operator produces at each pause point.
tour.languageLesson: Closed enums plus frozen JSON-Schema hashes make verdicts tamper-evident: the schema is pinned at a known hash, and any drift in the card shape is caught against that pin, so a recorded operator decision cannot be silently mutated after the fact.

tour.title: The Specialist Scaffold
tour.description: Every specialist agent is built from one canonical 9-node LangGraph shape: receive, plan, act, verify, reflect, emit_spans, gate_decision, finalize, handoff. The scaffold graph defines that lifecycle and the contract enforces conformance across all specialists via a discovery/build pattern. This is the single most important abstraction in the codebase, once you understand this template, every specialist in the next steps is a variation on it.
tour.languageLesson: LangGraph models an agent as a directed graph of node functions sharing a typed state object. A canonical scaffold plus a conformance contract lets dozens of specialists share one node-walk while customizing only the 'act' step, the framework wires receive->...->handoff identically for all of them.

tour.title: Irene: Two-Pass Figure-Grounded Authoring
tour.description: Irene is the flagship specialist, authoring narration in two passes with figure-grounding so the voiceover only speaks numbers and figures actually present on the slide. Pass-1 establishes the cluster/segment plan and Pass-2 fills the template, both built on the Step 5 scaffold. The shared figure-token helpers extract and normalize numeric/figure tokens that drive the source-fidelity audit, the mechanism that keeps narration honest.

tour.title: Production Specialists: Decks, Voice, Retrieval
tour.description: These three specialists turn plans into real assets by calling external providers. Gary decodes the envelope and dispatches Gamma to build slide decks; Enrique wires the voice-narration act around ElevenLabs; Texas enforces a sanctum lock and runs disciplined retrieval to fetch source material. All three reuse the Step 5 scaffold, differing only in their 'act' provider integration, which is the design payoff of a shared agent template.

tour.title: Quality Specialists: Vision & Quinn-R
tour.description: Two specialists guard fidelity. Vision performs slide-perception, reading rendered PNGs to verify what a learner would actually see, while Quinn-R assembles a review prompt and splits the LLM's response into structured fidelity-QA findings. Together they close the loop opened by Irene in Step 6: Vision checks the rendered output and Quinn-R judges whether the produced content faithfully matches its source.

tour.title: The Gated Workflow Substrate
tour.description: Gates are how human approval is interleaved with autonomous agent work. The resume API registers operator DecisionCards, computes their digests, and rebuilds run state to continue past a pause; the verdict module re-exports the operator-verdict vocabulary with import guards. This substrate is what makes the run pausable at G1/G2C/G3/G4 and resumable once the operator has decided, tying Step 4's cards to Step 3's state.

tour.title: Manifest-Compiled Pipeline
tour.description: Rather than hard-coding the run, the pipeline is declared as a manifest and compiled into a runnable LangGraph. The compiler wires specialist-dispatch nodes, gate pauses, and conditional lanes into one graph, while the manifest README marks the package as the loader/compiler scaffolding. This is where the specialists from Steps 6-8 and the gates from Step 9 are assembled into the end-to-end production walk.
tour.languageLesson: Compiling a declarative manifest into a graph (rather than imperatively wiring nodes) means the pipeline shape lives in data, not code. Changing the run order or inserting a gate is a manifest edit the compiler validates, which is why pack/manifest changes are governed as configuration.

tour.title: The Production Runner
tour.description: This is the engine that actually drives a trial: it walks the compiled LangGraph of specialists, pauses at each gate, and resumes after the operator's verdict. With a fan-out of 28 it is the highest-scope orchestration file in the project, pulling together state, specialists, gates, and the manifest from the previous steps. Note the two distinct node walks, the start walk and the resume/recover continuation, both of which must fire gate side-effects.

tour.title: Conversational SPOC Interlocution
tour.description: Newly evolved this run: Marcus now talks to the operator as a stop-and-chat single point of contact. The interlocutor is an LLM-driven REPL that narrates each gate's DecisionCard and lets the operator converse, while the SPOC CLI runs a scripted production trial gate by gate. A deterministic guard ensures the chatting model never drives the engine, the human's verdict, not the LLM's narration, advances the run.

tour.title: Research Citation & Workbook Producer
tour.description: Two recently added value-producing legs. The research wiring selects a retrieval posture and dispatches Texas (Step 7), then research_citation mints citation IDs, hashes sources, and gates entries so claims in the content are backed by real, auditable references. The workbook producer emits a learner workbook in Markdown and DOCX from the collateral specs, auditing numeric and citation fidelity along the way, the client-facing companion to the narrated deck.

tour.title: Audit Ledger & Database Schema
tour.description: Durable provenance lives in Postgres. The ledger emitter writes append-only learning events with idempotent insert semantics, and the schema.sql defines the ledger_events table (trial_id, gate_id, kind, JSONB payload) it writes to. This is the system's memory: every operator verdict and specialist outcome from the run becomes a queryable, tamper-resistant record, complementing the in-flight DecisionCards from Step 4.
tour.languageLesson: An append-only event table with idempotent inserts gives you an audit log that is safe to retry: replaying the same event is a no-op, so crashes mid-run never produce duplicates or corrupt the historical record. JSONB payloads keep the schema stable while allowing per-event-kind detail.

tour.title: Runtime, MCP Server & Configuration
tour.description: Finally, how the system is hosted and configured. The FastAPI runtime exposes an invoke endpoint with a Postgres status probe over the minimal graph, the MCP server scaffold surfaces gate-decision tools to MCP clients, and .env.example documents every API key and connection setting (OpenAI, ElevenLabs, Gamma, Postgres, Scite) the system needs to run. This closes the tour: from the agent roster in Step 1 to the live transport that serves the whole production runner.
